level_signals: 5+ years of experience in Product Management required
Expected to lead a dedicated squad and own long-term product strategy

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You have 5+ years of experience in Product Management, ideally within a high-growth SaaS, E-commerce, or Fintech environment.
must_haves.qualifier: ideally within a high-growth SaaS, E-commerce, or Fintech environment

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: You are highly analytical and comfortable using data to validate hypotheses, track performance, and influence stakeholder decisions.
You possess a deep understanding of product strategy and have a proven ability to write comprehensive feature specifications.
You are experienced in conducting user research and merchant interviews to uncover latent needs and market opportunities.
You are a clear and proactive communicator capable of managing complex dependencies and building consensus across diverse teams.
You are passionate about building scalable tools that empower small business owners and entrepreneurs to succeed.

nice_to_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Experience with booking or scheduling platforms

scope.surface: Bookings product within the Sell Online portfolio (appointment management, scheduling, multi-calendar, deposits)
scope.business_model: B2SMB SaaS — tools for micro-merchants and small businesses to manage appointments and sell services online
primary_metrics_inferred: Merchant adoption and retention of Bookings product
Feature delivery cadence and roadmap execution
Revenue growth from Bookings product line
Scaling to support larger businesses / specialized verticals (Beauty & Wellness)
core_responsibilities: Develop and execute the long-term product strategy and vision for the Bookings ecosystem in alignment with organizational goals.
Own the roadmap for multi-platform appointment management tools, ensuring merchants have a seamless experience across web and mobile.
Identify and close critical feature gaps for a rapidly scaling merchant base, with a specific focus on advanced scheduling and business automation.
Synthesize qualitative merchant feedback and quantitative data into actionable product requirements that address core user pain points.
Lead cross-functional teams of engineers and designers to prioritize and ship high-impact features on a consistent cadence.
Establish and monitor success metrics for the Bookings product line, using data-driven insights to iterate on the user experience and drive business growth.

compensation.equity_form: VSOP program — virtual stock options in SumUp's future success

green_flags: Clear product surface and ownership area — dedicated squad with specific mandate to scale Bookings
Explicit mention of VSOP equity program for upside participation
Annual L&D budget of €2000 stated explicitly
Generous PTO (26 days + public holidays + special leave days) and 1-month sabbatical after 3 years
JD explicitly encourages applications even if candidates don't meet 100% of criteria
Well-defined product moment — scaling from micro-merchants to larger businesses is a concrete mission
red_flags: Location not stated anywhere in the JD — city, country, remote/hybrid policy all absent
Visa sponsorship and work authorization not addressed
Benefits quoted in EUR (€2000 L&D), suggesting European base, but no explicit geography confirmed

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.manages_people (inferred as false — JD says 'lead cross-functional teams' but no direct reports mentioned)
location.type set to 'unclear' — no office/remote/hybrid policy stated anywhere in JD
scope.business_model (inferred from product description and SMB focus)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (not explicitly stated; inferred from responsibilities)
compensation.source set to 'unknown' — no salary range provided
ambiguities: Location is entirely absent from the JD body. Benefits are denominated in EUR (€2000 L&D) and other benefits (Multisport Card, PPK Employee Capital Plan) suggest a Central/Eastern European country (likely Poland), but this is not confirmed. work_auth, visa_sponsorship, and cities_listed all left as unclear/empty.

The 5+ years requirement is phrased under 'You'll thrive in this role if', which could be interpreted as preferred rather than hard-required. However, the specificity ('5+ years') and overall framing of the section as core qualifications led to classification as hard severity. Flagged as ambiguity.
